weather_report:
Swanbourne settles under a clean, untroubled sky tonight, the day folded neatly into a quiet, glassy dark. Stars feel nearer than usual, streetlights with no halo, the air spare and honest. The sun’s bite is long gone and with it the UV; what remains is a calm clarity that makes you pause on the step and listen to the hush of the lane.

The afternoon and early evening were bright and unblemished, a blue dome from end to end, the sun confident and UV lively while it rode high; now it has eased to nothing and that spotless feel carries into the night. Expect it to stay clear. It’s pleasant more than warm, so a light jacket or soft jumper will suit a brief wander. Good time to take a gentle loop around the block, linger by the gate while the dog investigates the hedgerow, or step into the garden to check the seedlings and let the sky rinse the day from your head before turning in. Crack a window for a thread of fresh air; let the stillness do the rest.
